shortDescription.text: Type mismatch between query filter and document causes update bypass

message.text: Server-Side JavaScript Injection: eval() is called directly on user-supplied input `req.body.preTax`. An attacker can submit arbitrary JavaScript code (e.g., `require('child_process').exec('...')`) as the preTax field value, achieving full Remote Code Execution on the server. The isNaN() validation on line 42 occurs AFTER eval() has already executed the payload, so it provides no protection.

Server-Side JavaScript Injection: eval() is called directly on user-supplied input `req.body.afterTax`. Same vulnerability as preTax — any of the three fields can independently serve as an RCE vector.
Server-Side JavaScript Injection: eval() is called directly on user-supplied input `req.body.roth`. Same vulnerability as preTax — any of the three fields can independently serve as an RCE vector.

MongoDB type mismatch in update query: The query filter uses `{userId}` (session value, likely a string), while the replacement document sets `userId: parseInt(userId)` (integer). If existing documents store userId as a string, the query matches but replaces it with an integer, causing getByUserId lookups with the original string type to miss the document. Conversely, if no document matches the string query, upsert creates a new document with the integer userId, leading to orphaned records. This can be exploited to create duplicate contribution records or cause denial of service by corrupting data lookups.
